phase_name: notmuch new.ignore guard for non-mail top-level entries

objectives.description: Add anchored regex entries to notmuch.nix new.ignore list
Confirm anchored /^...$/ form used (not bare basenames)
Rebuild: home-manager build / nix flake check
Optional live check: notmuch new --no-hooks before/after count

deviations.description: Optional live check: notmuch new --no-hooks --full-scan before/after 'Ignoring non-mail file' count
deviations.reason: Activation (home-manager switch) is out of scope for this implement run per the plan's own optional-live-check escape hatch. Verified instead via direct inspection of the built hm_notmuchdefaultconfig store derivation output, confirming the generated notmuch config's [new] ignore= line contains all 15 new anchored entries alongside the original 4 -- a stronger static guarantee than the live count check.
deviations.annotation: *(deviation: deferred -- activation/home-manager switch is out of scope for this implement run per the plan's own escape hatch; verified instead via direct inspection of the built hm_notmuchdefaultconfig store output, which is a stronger static guarantee than the optional live count check)*

notes: Added 15 anchored /^...$/ new.ignore regex entries to modules/home/email/notmuch.nix (lines 66-71 region), keeping the original 4 bare-name entries. home-manager build --flake .#benjamin succeeded (rebuilt hm_notmuchdefaultconfig, home-manager-files, home-manager-generation). nix flake check passed cleanly (all checks passed!). Inspected the built /nix/store/.../hm_notmuchdefaultconfig output directly (via nix-store -q --outputs on the matching .drv) and confirmed the [new] ignore= line contains: .mbsyncstate;.strstrings;.lock;dovecot*;/^specs$/;/^\.claude$/;/^\.git$/;/^docs$/;/^email_plans$/;/^email_reports$/;/^\.memory$/;/^README\.md$/;/^README_OLD\.md$/;/^CLAUDE\.md$/;/^Contacts$/;/^\.gitignore$/;/^\.logos-backup.*$/;/^\.logos-presync.*$/;/^\.syncstate-backups$/ -- exact match to the plan's specified list. The optional live notmuch-new count check was deferred since it requires activation (home-manager switch), which is out of scope for a build-only implement run; the static config-output inspection is a stronger and equally conclusive verification.
